competition.title: Pattern Recognition: Classic Presentations
competition.description: Recognize classic clinical patterns and syndromes instantly

questions.question: 22-year-old soccer player with immediate lateral ankle pain after inversion injury. Tender over ATFL, positive anterior drawer test.
questions.explanation: Classic ATFL injury with positive instability test in young athlete.

questions.question: 45-year-old office worker with shoulder pain during overhead activities. Painful arc 60-120°, positive Hawkins test.
questions.explanation: Painful arc and positive Hawkins test are classic signs of subacromial impingement.

questions.question: 35-year-old runner with morning heel pain, worse after rest. Point tenderness at medial calcaneal tuberosity.
questions.explanation: Classic morning pain and medial heel tenderness are hallmarks of plantar fasciitis.

questions.question: 28-year-old tennis player with lateral elbow pain. Tenderness over lateral epicondyle, pain with resisted wrist extension.
questions.explanation: Tennis elbow with classic presentation and positive resisted wrist extension test.

questions.question: 19-year-old student with anterior knee pain worse going down stairs and after prolonged sitting. Positive J-sign.
questions.explanation: Classic PFPS presentation with cinema sign and positive J-sign.

questions.question: 50-year-old with numbness in thumb and first two fingers, worse at night. Positive Tinel's sign at wrist.
questions.explanation: Classic CTS with nocturnal symptoms and median nerve distribution.

questions.question: 25-year-old weightlifter with acute lower back pain after deadlifting. Pain with forward flexion, no neurological signs.
questions.explanation: Acute mechanical pain without neurological involvement in young athlete.

questions.question: 65-year-old with gradual hip pain and morning stiffness <30 minutes. Reduced internal rotation, crepitus.
questions.explanation: Classic OA presentation with morning stiffness and reduced internal rotation.

questions.question: 42-year-old after MVA with neck pain and headaches. Reduced cervical ROM, upper trap spasm.
questions.explanation: Recent MVA with typical WAD presentation including headaches.

questions.question: 40-year-old runner with sudden calf pain during sprint. Palpable defect in medial gastrocnemius.
questions.explanation: Acute onset with palpable defect indicates muscle strain.

questions.question: 55-year-old with knee pain and catching sensation. Joint line tenderness, positive McMurray test, mild effusion.
questions.explanation: Mechanical symptoms with positive McMurray test indicate meniscal pathology.

questions.question: 38-year-old with lower back pain radiating to leg. Positive SLR at 45°, weakness in big toe extension.
questions.explanation: L5 nerve root compression with characteristic weakness pattern.

questions.question: 29-year-old basketball player with groin pain. Pain with resisted hip adduction, positive squeeze test.
questions.explanation: Specific adductor testing confirms adductor muscle involvement.

questions.question: 52-year-old with symmetrical hand joint swelling in MCPs and PIPs. Morning stiffness >1 hour.
questions.explanation: Symmetrical inflammatory pattern with prolonged morning stiffness.

questions.question: 44-year-old runner with lateral hip pain when lying on side. Positive Trendelenburg test.
questions.explanation: Lateral hip pain with positive Trendelenburg indicates GTPS.

questions.question: 35-year-old swimmer with anterior shoulder pain. Positive Speed's test, pain with resisted shoulder flexion.
questions.explanation: Anterior pain with positive Speed's test indicates biceps involvement.

questions.question: 22-year-old runner with medial shin pain during activity. Diffuse tenderness along medial tibial border.
questions.explanation: Classic shin splints with diffuse medial tibial tenderness.

questions.question: 30-year-old with unilateral headaches and neck stiffness. Pain originates from occiput.
questions.explanation: Occipital origin with neck involvement indicates cervicogenic headache.

questions.question: 45-year-old golfer with medial elbow pain. Pain with resisted wrist flexion and pronation.
questions.explanation: Golfer's elbow with characteristic pain pattern and resistance testing.

questions.question: 28-year-old runner with lateral ankle pain behind malleolus. Pain with resisted eversion.
questions.explanation: Location and resistance testing confirm peroneal tendon involvement.

questions.question: 40-year-old with sudden neck pain after sleeping awkwardly. Unable to turn head right, restricted rotation.
questions.explanation: Acute onset with rotational restriction suggests facet joint involvement.

questions.question: 24-year-old volleyball player with posterior shoulder pain during cocking phase. Positive relocation test.
questions.explanation: Posterior pain in overhead athlete with positive relocation test.

questions.question: 60-year-old with buttock pain and leg claudication when walking. Relief when leaning forward on shopping cart.
questions.explanation: Classic neurogenic claudication with forward flexion relief.

questions.question: 16-year-old gymnast with anterior knee pain. Pain with squatting, tender tibial tuberosity.
questions.explanation: Adolescent with tibial tuberosity tenderness indicates Osgood-Schlatter.

questions.question: 32-year-old dancer with posterior ankle pain. Pain with plantarflexion, tender posterior to lateral malleolus.
questions.explanation: Posterior pain with plantarflexion in dancer suggests impingement.

questions.question: 26-year-old pianist with thumb-side wrist pain. Positive Finkelstein's test, pain with thumb movement.
questions.explanation: Positive Finkelstein's test is pathognomonic for De Quervain's.

questions.question: 58-year-old with hip pain and limping. Thomas test positive, FABER test reproduces groin pain.
questions.explanation: Positive Thomas and FABER tests suggest hip flexor and capsular involvement.

questions.question: 34-year-old rock climber with finger pain. Unable to fully flex finger, tender A1 pulley.
questions.explanation: A1 pulley tenderness with catching indicates trigger finger.

questions.question: 27-year-old with intermittent lower back pain and morning stiffness >1 hour. Pain improves with activity.
questions.explanation: Young adult with inflammatory pattern suggests ankylosing spondylitis.

questions.question: 23-year-old dancer with lateral foot pain. Pain with single-leg stance, tender over 5th metatarsal base.
questions.explanation: 5th metatarsal base tenderness in dancer suggests Jones fracture.

questions.question: 45-year-old diabetic with burning foot pain at night. Stocking distribution numbness.
questions.explanation: Stocking distribution burning pain in diabetic indicates neuropathy.

questions.question: 62-year-old with shoulder pain and hand weakness. Positive Adson's test, paresthesias in C8-T1 distribution.
questions.explanation: Positive Adson's test with C8-T1 symptoms indicates TOS.

questions.question: 19-year-old female runner with hip pain and altered gait. Positive Trendelenburg, weak hip abductors.
questions.explanation: Positive Trendelenburg with weakness indicates gluteus medius dysfunction.

questions.question: 38-year-old with elbow pain and hand numbness. Positive elbow flexion test, tingling in ring and little finger.
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve distribution symptoms with positive elbow flexion test.

questions.question: 55-year-old with neck pain and arm weakness. Hoffman's sign positive, hyperreflexia.
questions.explanation: Upper motor neuron signs indicate cervical myelopathy.

questions.question: 28-year-old with foot pain between 3rd and 4th toes. Positive Mulder's click, forefoot pain.
questions.explanation: Interdigital pain with Mulder's click indicates Morton's neuroma.

questions.question: 41-year-old with calf pain and swelling after long flight. Positive Homan's sign, unilateral edema.
questions.explanation: Post-flight unilateral swelling with Homan's sign suggests DVT.

questions.question: 17-year-old football player with back pain. Step-off palpable at L5, pain with extension.
questions.explanation: Palpable step-off with extension pain indicates spondylolisthesis.

questions.question: 33-year-old with thumb pain at base. Positive grind test, pain with pinch grip.
questions.explanation: Positive grind test at thumb base indicates CMC joint arthritis.

questions.question: 25-year-old with knee pain and giving way. Positive pivot shift test, negative Lachman's test.
questions.explanation: Positive pivot shift with negative Lachman suggests PLC injury.

questions.question: 36-year-old with forearm pain during activity. Pain 5cm distal to lateral epicondyle, weak finger extension.
questions.explanation: Distal forearm pain with finger extension weakness indicates PIN syndrome.

questions.question: 24-year-old gymnast with wrist pain on thumb side. Pain with resisted thumb extension and abduction.
questions.explanation: Proximal to De Quervain's with crepitus indicates intersection syndrome.

questions.question: 52-year-old with heel pain medially and distally. Burning sensation, positive Tinel's sign behind medial malleolus.
questions.explanation: Posterior tibial nerve entrapment with positive Tinel's at tarsal tunnel.

questions.question: 29-year-old weightlifter with lateral knee pain. ITB tight, positive Ober's test, pain over lateral epicondyle.
questions.explanation: Lateral knee pain with tight ITB and positive Ober's test.

questions.question: 48-year-old with buttock pain and leg symptoms. Positive FAIR test, tenderness over piriformis.
questions.explanation: Positive FAIR test with piriformis tenderness indicates piriformis syndrome.

questions.question: 21-year-old dancer with medial ankle pain. Pain with forced dorsiflexion, tender posteromedial ankle.
questions.explanation: Posteromedial pain with dorsiflexion in dancer suggests impingement.

questions.question: 37-year-old with shoulder pain and weakness. Positive belly press test, difficulty with internal rotation.
questions.explanation: Positive belly press test indicates subscapularis muscle involvement.

questions.question: 15-year-old swimmer with shoulder pain. Positive sulcus sign, apprehension with abduction/external rotation.
questions.explanation: Positive sulcus sign indicates inferior laxity and MDI.

questions.question: 43-year-old with hand numbness at night. Symptoms in thumb, index, and middle finger only.
questions.explanation: Classic median nerve distribution indicates carpal tunnel syndrome.

questions.question: 31-year-old with foot pain on lateral border. Pain with inversion, tender over 5th metatarsal shaft.
questions.explanation: Lateral foot pain with metatarsal shaft tenderness indicates stress fracture.

questions.question: 56-year-old with burning shoulder pain at night. Weakness in shoulder abduction and external rotation.
questions.explanation: Night pain with weakness indicates significant rotator cuff pathology.

questions.question: 22-year-old with chronic ankle instability. Multiple previous sprains, positive anterior drawer and talar tilt.
questions.explanation: History of multiple sprains with positive instability tests indicates CAI.

questions.question: 39-year-old with lateral elbow pain radiating to forearm. Pain with middle finger extension against resistance.
questions.explanation: Middle finger extension test positive indicates radial tunnel syndrome.

questions.question: 18-year-old basketball player with knee pain below patella. Tender patellar tendon, pain with jumping.
questions.explanation: Patellar tendon tenderness with jumping pain indicates tendinopathy.

questions.question: 47-year-old with gradual onset hand stiffness. Nodular thickening of palmar fascia, finger contracture.
questions.explanation: Palmar fascial thickening with contracture indicates Dupuytren's disease.

questions.question: 26-year-old runner with medial knee pain. Pain along medial joint line, worse with valgus stress.
questions.explanation: Medial joint line pain with valgus stress indicates MCL injury.

questions.question: 64-year-old with progressive back pain and leg claudication. Symptoms worse with extension, better with flexion.
questions.explanation: Neurogenic claudication with extension intolerance indicates stenosis.

questions.question: 32-year-old with thumb pain at CMC joint. Positive grind test, pain with key pinch.
questions.explanation: CMC joint involvement with positive grind test indicates arthritis.

questions.question: 44-year-old with hip pain and clicking. Pain with hip flexion, positive FABER test.
questions.explanation: Mechanical symptoms with positive FABER suggest labral pathology.

questions.question: 29-year-old with foot pain over 2nd metatarsal head. Pain with weight bearing, callus formation.
questions.explanation: Metatarsal head pain with callus indicates metatarsalgia.

questions.question: 35-year-old violinist with neck pain and arm symptoms. Positive elevated arm stress test, C8-T1 distribution.
questions.explanation: Elevated arm stress test positive with C8-T1 symptoms indicates TOS.

questions.question: 19-year-old gymnast with wrist pain during weight bearing. Pain over scaphoid, positive scaphoid shift test.
questions.explanation: Positive scaphoid shift test indicates scapholunate instability.

questions.question: 42-year-old with progressive foot deformity. Pes planus, weakness in tibialis posterior, too many toes sign.
questions.explanation: Progressive flatfoot with too many toes sign indicates PTTD.

questions.question: 16-year-old dancer with hip pain and limited internal rotation. Positive anterior impingement test.
questions.explanation: Limited internal rotation with impingement test indicates FAI.

questions.question: 38-year-old with elbow pain and weak grip. Positive chair test, pain with resisted supination.
questions.explanation: Chair test and resisted supination pain indicate lateral epicondylitis.

questions.question: 51-year-old with shoulder pain and weakness. Positive drop arm test, external rotation lag sign.
questions.explanation: Positive drop arm and lag signs indicate rotator cuff tear.

questions.question: 27-year-old with knee pain and instability. Positive posterior drawer, posterior sag sign.
questions.explanation: Posterior instability tests indicate PCL injury.

questions.question: 33-year-old with chronic back pain. Pain worse in morning, improves with activity, positive Schober's test.
questions.explanation: Inflammatory pattern with reduced spinal mobility indicates AS.

questions.question: 24-year-old with heel pain and morning stiffness. Bilateral involvement, family history of autoimmune disease.
questions.explanation: Bilateral heel pain with family history suggests spondyloarthropathy.

questions.question: 45-year-old with hand numbness and weakness. Positive Froment's sign, weakness in finger abduction.
questions.explanation: Froment's sign and intrinsic weakness indicate ulnar nerve compression.

questions.question: 28-year-old pitcher with shoulder pain in late cocking phase. Positive relocation test, apprehension at 90° abduction.
questions.explanation: Apprehension and positive relocation test indicate anterior instability.

questions.question: 17-year-old soccer player with knee pain and swelling. Positive Lachman's test, positive pivot shift.
questions.explanation: Positive Lachman's and pivot shift indicate ACL rupture.

questions.question: 52-year-old with progressive hand weakness. Thenar muscle wasting, positive Phalen's test.
questions.explanation: Thenar wasting with positive Phalen's indicates severe CTS.

questions.question: 36-year-old with foot pain between toes. Positive squeeze test, pain with toe dorsiflexion.
questions.explanation: Toe dorsiflexion pain with squeeze test indicates plantar plate injury.

questions.question: 41-year-old with hip pain and limp. Positive Thomas test, weakness in hip flexion.
questions.explanation: Positive Thomas test with flexion weakness indicates hip flexor injury.

questions.question: 29-year-old with ankle pain and giving way. Positive anterior drawer, negative talar tilt.
questions.explanation: Isolated anterior drawer indicates specific ATFL injury.

questions.question: 48-year-old with neck pain and arm weakness. Positive Spurling's test, C6 dermatome numbness.
questions.explanation: Positive Spurling's with C6 distribution indicates nerve root compression.

questions.question: 23-year-old with elbow pain and tingling in little finger. Positive elbow flexion test, Tinel's at cubital tunnel.
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve symptoms with positive tests indicate cubital tunnel syndrome.

questions.question: 37-year-old with chronic back pain radiating to buttock. Positive Patrick's test, sacroiliac joint tenderness.
questions.explanation: Positive Patrick's test with SI joint tenderness indicates SIJ dysfunction.

questions.question: 31-year-old with wrist pain on ulnar side. Positive TFCC load test, pain with ulnar deviation.
questions.explanation: Positive TFCC load test indicates triangular fibrocartilage complex injury.

questions.question: 26-year-old tennis player with wrist pain during backhand. Pain over dorsal wrist, positive scapholunate ballottement.
questions.explanation: Dorsal wrist pain with positive ballottement indicates SL ligament injury.

questions.question: 54-year-old with shoulder pain and night symptoms. Positive Neer's sign, painful arc 60-120°.
questions.explanation: Classic impingement signs with painful arc indicate subacromial pathology.

questions.question: 19-year-old gymnast with back pain during extension. Positive single-leg extension test, L5 pars defect on imaging.
questions.explanation: Extension pain with pars defect indicates spondylolysis.

questions.question: 43-year-old with knee pain and clicking during squatting. Positive McMurray's test, joint line tenderness.
questions.explanation: Mechanical symptoms with positive McMurray's indicate meniscal pathology.

options: Chronic ankle instability with peroneal weakness
questions.question: 35-year-old with chronic ankle instability and lateral ankle pain. Positive anterior drawer, peroneal weakness.
questions.explanation: Combination of instability and peroneal weakness indicates complex CAI.
questions.correctAnswer: Chronic ankle instability with peroneal weakness

questions.question: 28-year-old climber with finger pain and swelling. Positive bowstring test, tender A2 pulley.
questions.explanation: Bowstring test and A2 tenderness indicate pulley injury in climber.

questions.question: 46-year-old with hip pain and limited flexion. Positive FABER test, groin pain with internal rotation.
questions.explanation: Limited motion with groin pain indicates hip joint pathology.

questions.question: 22-year-old with foot pain during push-off phase of running. Pain over 1st MTP joint, limited dorsiflexion.
questions.explanation: Limited MTP dorsiflexion with pain indicates hallux rigidus.

questions.question: 39-year-old with lateral elbow pain and weak wrist extension. Positive middle finger test, pain 5cm distal to lateral epicondyle.
questions.explanation: Middle finger test with distal pain indicates radial tunnel syndrome.

questions.question: 57-year-old with progressive shoulder weakness. Positive horn blower's sign, weakness in external rotation.
questions.explanation: Horn blower's sign indicates teres minor muscle involvement.

questions.question: 24-year-old dancer with medial knee pain. Pain along pes anserine insertion, tender 2cm below joint line.
questions.explanation: Pes anserine location with specific tenderness indicates bursitis.

questions.question: 48-year-old with chronic neck pain and headaches. Positive upper cervical flexion test, suboccipital tenderness.
questions.explanation: Upper cervical tests and suboccipital findings indicate C1-C2 dysfunction.

questions.question: 33-year-old with thumb pain at base. Positive CMC grind test, pain with jar opening.
questions.explanation: CMC grind test with functional limitation indicates arthritis.

questions.question: 27-year-old with lower back pain after deadlifting. Positive prone instability test, pain with lumbar extension.
questions.explanation: Positive instability test indicates segmental control deficit.

questions.question: 41-year-old with heel pain and morning stiffness. Windlass test positive, pain with passive big toe extension.
questions.explanation: Positive windlass test specifically indicates plantar fascia involvement.

questions.question: 18-year-old volleyball player with shoulder pain during spiking. Positive O'Brien's test, pain deep in shoulder.
questions.explanation: O'Brien's test indicates superior labral anterior-posterior tear.

questions.question: 52-year-old with progressive hand deformity. Swan neck deformity of fingers, boutonniere deformity.
questions.explanation: Classic RA deformities indicate advanced inflammatory arthritis.

questions.question: 34-year-old with lateral hip pain during running. Positive single-leg squat test, weak hip abductors.
questions.explanation: Single-leg squat test with weakness indicates gluteus medius pathology.

questions.question: 26-year-old with foot pain during cutting movements. Lisfranc joint tenderness, positive plantar flexion test.
questions.explanation: Lisfranc joint pain with positive testing indicates midfoot injury.

questions.question: 45-year-old with progressive elbow pain and deformity. Positive lateral pivot shift test of elbow, posterolateral instability.
questions.explanation: Lateral pivot shift test indicates complex posterolateral instability.

instructions: You have 5 minutes to answer as many questions correctly as possible
Questions must be answered consecutively - one wrong answer ends the game
Your score equals the number of consecutive correct answers
Click 'Start Challenge' when ready to begin
